title: Step 2: Click the  button in the past queries pane.

sections.heading: Save a Favorite Query
sections.body:
You can favorite a query for future use. If you load a favorite query, you can
change it without changing the original saved copy.

To add a query to your favorites:

Click the clock  icon on the query bar in the
Documents pane.

Hover over your query and click the star Star button.

Enter a name for your query.

sections.heading: View Saved Queries
sections.body:
After you connect to your cluster, you can view your saved queries and
aggregation pipelines on the **My Queries** tab.

> **Note:** To learn how to save and view aggregation pipelines, see:
>
> - [Save a Pipeline](compass/agg-pipeline-builder/save-agg-pipeline)
>
> - [Open a Saved Pipeline](compass/agg-pipeline-builder/open-saved-pipeline)

sections.heading: Use the My Queries Tab
sections.body:
When you click a saved or favorite query tile, Atlas opens the
**Documents** tab with the filter loaded.

In the **My Queries** tab, you can also click a [saved aggregation](atlas-ui-save-agg-pipeline) pipeline tile to open the
**Aggregations** tab with the pipeline loaded.

sections.heading: Use the Favorites Tab
sections.body:
You can also view favorite queries from the **Favorites** tab
from in the **Documents** view. To open the
**Favorites** tab:

Click the clock  icon at the top of the
Documents tab.

Select your favorited operation.

- If your favorited operation is a query, the Favorites list
  view only displays a Filter statement, copy 
  icon, and delete  icon.

  > **Note:** Clicking a favorited query in the
  > Favorites list view populates your
  > Query Bar with that query.

- If your favorited operation is a bulk update statement, the
  Favorites list view only displays a
  Filter statement, Update statement, copy
   icon, delete  icon, and open new tab
   icon.

  > **Note:** If you click the open new tab  icon, the
  > Update Documents modal opens with the bulk
  > update statement and the filter criteria. For details on bulk
  > update statements in Atlas, see
  > [Modify Multiple Documents](atlas-ui-bulk-update).

sections.heading: View Query History
sections.body:
For details on how to view query history see
[viewing recent query history](atlas-ui-recent-queries).
